enrichment: none — typography + a contribution graph built from real release data
brief: Image Horse · release log. Segmented month filter (2026 label, large month + rel count, release squares) over 82 releases. Each month opens with an achievement row: features/releases/commits/fixes counted from the data, the two biggest perks picked by feature-count, and that month’s COMMIT graph from git log (211 commits / 42 active days) on the right.
data: releases.js (82, from marketing/src/pages/Trail.tsx) + commits.js (211, from git log at the repo root)

brief: Image Horse · pricing. The $0 figure is the argument (WASM runs on the visitor's CPU), paired with a worded headline per gate 46. Tier cards + the real access matrix.

Image Horse · full system reference. Two-plane map (solid client / dashed optional network, severable seam) + the complete stack from the old marketing page: 9 WASM modules, Convex functions, storage, AI models, webhooks, and the 6-table Convex schema (58 fields, 14 indexes). system-architecture.mermaid ships as a download — mermaid cannot parse OKLCH, so a live render would mean a second hex palette plus ~1MB of CDN.

diversification: SUSPENDED — user explicitly requested the usehallmark.com/examples/hyperlane fingerprint
brief: Image Horse · browser image editor, Rust/WASM, local-first

_note: This is now a multi-page site. Nav (N5) / footer (Ft5) / theme (Bloom) are deliberately SHARED across every page — the per-run diversification rule does not apply within one site, only the macrostructure rotates per page type. Say 'lock the system' to formalise this as a design.md.
